intro: Monteverdia purpusii, also known as the Mexican cherry or yellow guava, is a small, evergreen tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Monteverdia purpusii is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
